label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's look through the text to find which kind of fish is best for you according to the author. The first paragraph says "women who ate five or more dishes of baked fish a month had a thirty percent lower risk of heart failure in comparison to women who ate less than one dish a month." But it doesn't specify which fish here. The next paragraph says "The women who had eaten lots of baked fish like salmon, were more healthy than those who mostly ate tuna or white fish." So baked salmon is mentioned as being associated with more health benefits than tuna or white fish. Then it says "Eating baked fish also appeared to provide a protection against high blood pressure." Again, not specifying a particular type yet. The text later says "this study also showed that just by eating one dish of fried fish a week led to a 49% greater risk of heart failure, and eating fried fish led to lower fiber intake and higher calorie intake." So fried fish is worse. The final paragraph says the American Heart Association suggests eating a minimum of two dishes of fish, especially fatty fish, each week. But this doesn't directly answer which type is best. Looking at the choices: A) Baked tuna - but the text says women who ate tuna were less healthy than those who ate baked salmon. B) Baked salmon - this is mentioned as being associated with more health benefits. C) Boiled salmon - the text only mentions baked fish,
